must_not_assert: that the operator was unauthorized or that a real-world compromise outside this capture is established
that EV-003 proves payload effects beyond the observed path-intercepted execution as SYSTEM
that the service and files were successfully removed or that persistence remained active after the capture window

the_trap: The Example Service naming, WindowsServiceExample original filename, and cleanup command can invite under-triage. But the unquoted service path is deliberately populated at C:\program.exe, the service is started, and EV-003 confirms that Windows resolves and executes C:\program.exe from services.exe as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
rationale: A0 creates a service with the unquoted path C:\Program Files\windows_service.exe. EV-001 places the same executable at both the intended path and the higher-priority C:\program.exe interception path, EV-002 starts the service, and EV-003 records C:\program.exe executing under services.exe as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM while its command line still names the intended Program Files path. This is decisive evidence that the unquoted-path interception succeeded with SYSTEM privileges. EV-004 is mitigating cleanup context, but it is only a stop/delete/cleanup command and does not undo the already observed privileged execution. Under this benchmark, malicious denotes decisive execution of an offensive security mechanism; it does not claim that the operator was unauthorized. Strong, malicious, high-critical; up-rank despite the example-binary and cleanup surface.
